coaching.coaching: Plan how binary pass/fail condition for AC1 verification will be verified in-session — restate any deferred or unobservable success target as an observable proxy.
coaching.subject: binary pass/fail condition for AC1 verification

coaching.coaching: Pin down optional-vs-mandatory decision on naming convention with a concrete worked example.
coaching.subject: optional-vs-mandatory decision on naming convention

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Harden AC #1's proving command to a binary assertion covering ALL named duplicated rules: replace `rg -c 'worktree|plan review|Track all work' CLAUDE.local.md` with `rg -c 'worktree|plan.review|venv|CI-before|rebar.track|Gerrit.workflow' CLAUDE.local.md` and change 'trends to 0' to 'must return 0 (no matches)'.
evidence: "trends to 0 for the generally-applicable rules"

Named rules in the Why section: 'worktree discipline, activate-the-venv, plan-review signing, CI-before-close, the rebar tracking loop, a restatement of the Gerrit workflow' — only 'worktree' and 'plan review' are in the regex
findings.finding: The proving command for AC #1 uses 'trends to 0' (a hedge) rather than a binary assertion, and its regex pattern ('worktree|plan review|Track all work') covers only a subset of the named duplicated rules — 'activate-the-venv', 'CI-before-close', 'rebar tracking loop', and 'Gerrit workflow' are absent from the pattern, so the command cannot prove the criterion fully.
scenarios: Executor removes only lines matching 'worktree|plan review|Track all work' but leaves 'activate-the-venv' or 'CI-before-close' guidance, the proving command still reports 0 and the AC appears satisfied while duplicated content remains.
The word 'trends' allows partial compliance to be accepted rather than requiring zero matches.
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace the proving line with:
```
Proving: rg -c 'worktree|plan.review|venv|CI-before|rebar.track|Gerrit.workflow' CLAUDE.local.md
# must exit 1 (no matches found) — i.e., count = 0
```
And replace 'trends to 0' with 'must equal 0'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten AC1's verify condition from 'trends to 0' to an exact threshold (e.g. 'must return 0; any match is a failure') so the criterion is binary and unambiguous.
evidence: "trends to 0 for the generally-applicable rules" — 'trends to' implies a direction, not a definite threshold, leaving the pass/fail bar ambiguous
findings.finding: AC1's verification condition 'trends to 0' is indeterminate: it is unclear whether the rg count must be exactly 0 (hard pass/fail) or merely lower than before (a directional improvement). This makes the criterion non-binary and untestable as written.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1 — verify step: `rg -c 'worktree|plan review|Track all work' CLAUDE.local.md` trends to 0
scenarios: Reviewer sees a count of 1 or 2 after the strip and must judge whether that 'trends to 0' — the criterion gives no resolution.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'trends to 0 for the generally-applicable rules' with 'must equal 0; any non-zero count is a failure (the file still contains general-purpose guidance).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the optionality of the @import modernization decision: either remove AC #3 (if truly optional) or remove 'not required by this story' / 'Optionally' from the body and state a default (e.g., 'default: keep current name unless investigation shows easy adoption').
evidence: "Optionally note that Anthropic now deprecates the CLAUDE.local.md name in favor of importing a gitignored file via @ — record whether we adopt that or keep the current name (a coherence decision, not required by this story)."
"Decision on the CLAUDE.local.md-name-vs-gitignored-@import modernization is recorded." — AC #3 has no 'optional' qualifier
findings.finding: The plan body marks the CLAUDE.local.md-name-vs-@import decision as 'Optional' ('Optionally note…'), but AC #3 makes it mandatory ('Decision… is recorded') — conflicting signals leave the executor uncertain whether skipping the decision is acceptable.
findings.location: What section ('Optionally note…') vs. Acceptance Criteria AC #3
scenarios: Executor reads 'not required by this story' in the body and skips the decision, then reviewer flags AC #3 as unmet.
Executor spends time researching @-import syntax unnecessarily when the intent was only to note either 'adopt' or 'keep current'.
findings.suggested_fix: Simplest fix: add '(default: keep current CLAUDE.local.md name; record that decision explicitly)' to both the body and AC #3, making clear the decision itself is required but a one-line record suffices.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a checked-in, non-gitignored target (e.g., an ADR file, AGENTS.md section, or CLAUDE.md note) where the CLAUDE.local.md-name-vs-@import decision will be recorded so it is visible to all operators and future agents.
evidence: "Decision on the `CLAUDE.local.md`-name-vs-gitignored-`@import` modernization is recorded." — no target document named
CLAUDE.local.md is git-ignored, so any decision recorded solely there is not visible to collaborators or CI
The @import pattern affects how all agents/operators discover local config — a coherence decision for the project
findings.finding: AC #3 mandates a decision record on the CLAUDE.local.md-name-vs-@import modernization, but the plan does not specify where this record is captured. If it is written only into the git-ignored CLAUDE.local.md, it is invisible to other operators and future agents, stranding the architectural decision.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #3 / 'Decision on the CLAUDE.local.md-name-vs-gitignored-@import modernization is recorded.'
scenarios: A second operator sets up the project, finds CLAUDE.local.md, and doesn't know whether the team decided to adopt @import or keep the current name — decision is lost.
A future plan author re-raises the same question, unaware a decision was already made.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC #3: 'Record the decision in a checked-in location (e.g., docs/adr/NNNN-local-agent-config-naming.md or a dedicated section in AGENTS.md) so all operators can discover it.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit pre-condition gate to AC2 (e.g. 'Before any removal, confirm the AGENTS.md story has merged by checking AGENTS.md for each rule; if the home is absent, block this story') so the dependency is enforced at execution time, not just noted in Scope.
evidence: 'Depends on: the AGENTS.md story (the generally-applicable rules must have a home before removal).'
AC2: 'Every rule removed has a verified home in AGENTS.md or docs/ (no guidance lost).' — no gate on story completion
The proving mechanism ('cite the AGENTS.md/docs location in the change description') is a post-hoc description, not a pre-execution guard
findings.finding: AC2 requires that 'every rule removed has a verified home in AGENTS.md or docs/' but the plan's own Scope section declares a hard dependency on a separate 'AGENTS.md story' completing first. The AC does not encode this as a hard pre-condition check, so an agent executing this plan in isolation could remove content from CLAUDE.local.md before the AGENTS.md story has actually landed, resulting in guidance loss with no safety net.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 / Scope section ('Depends on: the AGENTS.md story')
scenarios: Agent executes this plan before the AGENTS.md story merges; removes rules from CLAUDE.local.md; cites expected-but-not-yet-landed AGENTS.md locations; guidance is silently lost until the dependency lands.
findings.suggested_fix: Revise AC2 to read: 'Every rule removed has a verified, already-merged home in AGENTS.md or docs/. Pre-condition: run `rg '<rule-text>' AGENTS.md docs/` before removing each rule; if the match is absent, halt and resolve the AGENTS.md story first. Cite the location in the change description.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that the AGENTS.md file (the destination for removed rules) actually exists in the repo before this story is worked; if it does not yet exist, add an explicit sequencing guard (e.g., a `rebar` dependency link) that blocks this story from being claimed until the AGENTS.md story is closed.
evidence: Plan quote: 'OUT: adding new content to AGENTS.md/docs (that is the AGENTS.md and re-home stories — this story only removes what they already re-homed).'
Plan quote: 'Depends on: the AGENTS.md story (the generally-applicable rules must have a home before removal).'
Repo-wide search for 'AGENTS.md' returns zero hits for an actual file at the repo root; only plan-review cache entries and test identifiers reference it.
ABSENCE: no AGENTS.md file visible in list_directory('.') output.
findings.finding: The plan's scope-exclusion claim 'OUT: adding new content to AGENTS.md/docs (that is the AGENTS.md and re-home stories — this story only removes what they already re-homed)' and AC2 ('Every rule removed has a verified home in AGENTS.md or docs/') both rest on the premise that AGENTS.md exists (or will exist when this story executes). A repo-wide search finds no AGENTS.md file at the repo root or anywhere in the project tree — only references to it in plan-review cache files. If the AGENTS.md story has not yet landed when this story runs, the scope exclusion silently deletes work (the executor removes content from CLAUDE.local.md with no verified destination), making this a G-4 false-exclusion risk.
findings.location: Scope section ('OUT: adding new content to AGENTS.md/docs') and AC2 ('Every rule removed has a verified home in AGENTS.md or docs/')
scenarios: Executor runs this story before the AGENTS.md story lands; removes rules from CLAUDE.local.md citing 'they already have a home in AGENTS.md' but that file does not exist — guidance is permanently lost.
Reviewer attempts to verify AC2 ('cite the AGENTS.md/docs location in the change description') but cannot because AGENTS.md does not exist — AC2 cannot pass.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `rebar link <this-story-id> <agents-md-story-id> blocked_by` so the claim gate enforces ordering, and update the Scope section to cite the specific AGENTS.md section headings where each removed rule will live (e.g., 'worktree discipline → AGENTS.md §Working Environment').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a durable, visible location for the AC3 decision record (e.g., a comment on this rebar ticket, a note in `CONTRIBUTING.md`, or a commit message trailer) rather than leaving it implicitly in the git-ignored `CLAUDE.local.md`.
evidence: AC3 says only 'is recorded' with no target artifact named.
CLAUDE.local.md is git-ignored (.gitignore:18) and local — any content there is invisible to other agents/operators.
The parent epic (81e2) manages a family of shared-guidance changes; a local-only decision record would not propagate to that shared context.
Prior review cache (.rebar/cache/plan-review/955c-7a98-2485-4209/7545b3fd9ec25bf09979.json) independently flagged this same gap.
findings.finding: AC3 mandates recording a decision about the CLAUDE.local.md-name-vs-@import modernization, but the plan never specifies WHERE this record is captured. Since CLAUDE.local.md is git-ignored and machine-local, a decision written there is invisible to all other agents and operators — effectively losing the coherence record.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'Decision on the `CLAUDE.local.md`-name-vs-gitignored-`@import` modernization is recorded.'
scenarios: An implementer writes the decision into CLAUDE.local.md (the only file in scope), then another agent on a different machine working the sibling AGENTS.md story has no visibility into the naming decision and makes an inconsistent choice.
A future operator setting up a new machine has no checked-in record of whether the @import migration was adopted.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC3: 'Record the decision as a comment on this ticket (or in the commit message) so it is visible in the shared rebar store, not only in the local file.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale in the What/Scope section for why the @import modernization decision is deferred (e.g., 'Deferring reduces blast radius — adopting @import requires first verifying Claude Code's import-at-launch semantics, which belongs in the AGENTS.md spike; this story only removes content whose general home is already confirmed') so the scoping choice is justified rather than just asserted.
evidence: 'a coherence decision, not required by this story' — no affirmative reason given for deferral vs. atomic adoption

The @import alternative is explicitly surfaced as known but only marked 'Optionally note'; no positive rationale (e.g., 'deferring reduces blast radius because X', 'adoption requires verifying Claude Code @import semantics first') is stated
G6 criterion: 'the plan states a POSITIVE rationale for the chosen approach (its absence is a finding)' — this applies to scoping choices where a known alternative is acknowledged but deferred
findings.finding: The plan defers the @import modernization decision (CLAUDE.local.md-name-vs-gitignored-@import) as optional/out-of-scope but provides no positive rationale for WHY deferral is preferable to resolving it atomically in this story. G6 requires the plan to state a positive reason for its scoping choice when a known structural alternative is surfaced and not adopted.
findings.location: What section / Acceptance Criteria AC #3 — 'Optionally note that Anthropic now deprecates the CLAUDE.local.md name in favor of importing a gitignored file via @ — record whether we adopt that or keep the current name (a coherence decision, not required by this story).'
scenarios: An implementer seeing 'not required by this story' with no rationale may arbitrarily resolve the convention one way or the other, making an irreversible naming choice without understanding why the plan considered it deferred.
A reviewer evaluating the plan cannot judge whether the deferral is intentional architectural sequencing (e.g., pending spike results on @import semantics) or accidental under-scoping.
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, after the @import mention, add one sentence such as: 'We defer adoption because the @import semantic behavior (does it reduce context? does it chain?) should be confirmed by the AGENTS.md spike before this operator commits to a convention — keeping the current name is the zero-blast-radius default until that evidence exists.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
